name: devflow
description: End-to-end agent development process. Use when coordinating work, dispatching agents, or reviewing PRs.
capabilities: Defines 5-phase development workflow from assessment to merge
Enforces 100% issue requirement coverage verification at every phase
Coordinates parallel agent implementation with Docker-based dispatch
Implements 6-pass code review starting with mandatory coverage check
Integrates with GitHub CLI for issue and PR management
Requires Mermaid diagrams for documenting architectural changes
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only development workflow guidelines in markdown format. All 42 static findings are false positives: markdown code block delimiters (backticks) were misidentified as Ruby/shell command execution; workflow terminology like 'dispatch' and 'coordinate' triggered C2 false positives; git/github CLI command references triggered system reconnaissance false positives; and JSON metadata fields triggered cryptographic hash false positives. The skill contains no executable code, network calls, or file system access. The pre-computed skill-report.json already passed security audit with safe rating.
valueStatement: Development teams struggle with coordinating AI agents and ensuring complete issue coverage across complex projects. This skill provides a systematic 5-phase workflow that guarantees 100% requirement implementation through mandatory coverage verification at every step.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI for full functionality with issues and PRs
Assumes Docker environment for agent dispatch capability
Designed for Git-based workflows only, limited for other VCS
May be overly rigid for small or quick changes

useCases.title: Coordinate Multi-Agent Projects
useCases.description: Manage complex development tasks by dispatching multiple AI agents in parallel while ensuring complete requirement coverage across all workstreams

useCases.title: Systematic PR Review Process
useCases.description: Follow a structured 6-pass review process that starts with verifying 100% issue coverage before checking code quality or merging

useCases.title: Ensure Complete Feature Delivery
useCases.description: Track development progress with mandatory coverage gates that prevent merging until every requirement from the original issue is implemented

promptTemplates.title: Start Development Workflow
promptTemplates.scenario: Begin coordinated development on a new issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Use devflow to assess issue #123 and coordinate multi-agent development for implementing the user authentication feature

promptTemplates.title: Review PR with Coverage
promptTemplates.scenario: Review pull request with requirement verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply devflow Phase 3 review to PR #456, starting with 100% issue coverage verification before code quality checks

promptTemplates.title: Final Coverage Gate
promptTemplates.prompt: Run devflow Phase 4.5 final coverage gate on PR #789 to ensure all requirements from issue #321 are implemented before merge

promptTemplates.title: Complex Architecture Change
promptTemplates.prompt: Follow devflow for implementing the new microservices architecture and include Mermaid sequence diagrams in the PR for all state flows

bestPractices: Always start with Phase 1 Oracle Assessment to extract ALL requirements before any implementation begins
Use the 6-pass review process consistently, never skip the mandatory coverage verification pass 0.5
Include Mermaid diagrams in PRs when implementing flows, states, or any architectural changes
antiPatterns: Never merge PRs without first passing 100% issue coverage verification at Phase 4.5
Do not push directly to branches - always use feature branches with proper PR workflow
Avoid manual issue closing - use Closes #X syntax in PR body for automatic closure on merge

faq.question: Is devflow compatible with non-GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: Devflow is optimized for GitHub with CLI integration but can be adapted for other Git platforms with modified commands

faq.question: What happens if coverage verification fails?
faq.answer: The workflow returns to Phase 2 with a detailed gap list - merging is blocked until 100% coverage is achieved

faq.question: How does devflow integrate with existing CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Devflow works alongside CI/CD - Phase 3 review begins after CI passes but adds mandatory coverage verification before merge

faq.question: Is the 100% coverage requirement too strict for agile development?
faq.answer: The requirement ensures complete delivery - teams can split large issues into smaller tasks to maintain agility

faq.question: Can I use devflow for solo development without agents?
faq.answer: Yes, skip agent dispatch in Phase 2 and use devflow as a structured development and review checklist

faq.question: How does the Docker agent dispatch work?
faq.answer: Phase 2 uses Docker containers to run independent Claude agents in parallel, each working on assigned task subsets
